questions.title: Digital Logic || 2015 MCQ || 2-mark || Module 4: Sequential Circuit
questions.question: A positive edge-triggered D flip-flop is connected to a positive edge-triggered JK flipflop as follows. The Q output of the D flip-flop is connected to both the J and K inputs of the JK flip-flop, while the Q output of the JK flip-flop is connected to the input of the D flip-flop. Initially, the output of the D flip-flop is set to logic one and the output of the JK flip-flop is cleared. Which one of the following is the bit sequence (including the initial state) generated at the Q output of the JK flip-flop when the flip-flops are connected to a free-running common clock? Assume that J = K = 1 is the toggle mode and J = K = 0 is the state-holding mode of the JK flip-flop. Both the flip-flops have non-zero propagation delays. [GATE||2015||SET1||MCQ||2MARKS]
questions.question_html: A positive edge-triggered D flip-flop is connected to a positive edge-triggered JK flipflop as follows. The Q output of the D flip-flop is connected to both the J and K inputs of the JK flip-flop, while the Q output of the JK flip-flop is connected to the input of the D flip-flop. Initially, the output of the D flip-flop is set to logic one and the output of the JK flip-flop is cleared. Which one of the following is the bit sequence (including the initial state) generated at the Q output of the JK flip-flop when the flip-flops are connected to a free-running common clock? Assume that J = K = 1 is the toggle mode and J = K = 0 is the state-holding mode of the JK flip-flop. Both the flip-flops have non-zero propagation delays. [GATE||2015||SET1||MCQ||2MARKS]
questions.explanation:
. Initially Q output of D - FF  = 1 Initially Q output of JK - FF  = 0 Now with the help of present state and next state table we can see what is happening in circuit.
- Toggle: J = K = 1
- Hold : J = K = 0
We see from table Q output of D-FF is going to next state input of JK-FF and the bits sequence produced is like 110110….. Including initial condition (0) we get output as 0110110110…. Hence answer is (A) part. Another Explanation: Here, it is given that JK flip flop will toggle when J = K = 1 and it will retain the output if J = K = 0. Also, the output of the D flip flop would remain the same as the input. So, we have Initial output : D = 1
JK = 0
After clock 1 : D = 0 (D gets 0 as input from initial output of JK, so output = 0) JK = 1 (J = K = 1 from initial output of D, so output would be toggled from 0 to 1)
After clock 2 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as input from previous output of JK, so output = 1) JK = 1 (J = K = 0 from previous output of D, so output would be retained to 1)
After clock 3 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 1) JK = 0 (J = K = 1 from previous output of D, so output would be toggled from 1 to 0)
After clock 4 : D = 0 (D gets 0 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 0) JK = 1 (J = K = 1 from previous output of D, so output would be toggled from 0 to 1)
After clock 5 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 1) JK = 1 (J = K = 0 from previous output of D, so output would be retained to 1)
Thus, the bit sequence generated at the Q output of the JK flip flop will be 0110110...
questions.explanation_html:
Initially Q output of D - FF  = 1 Initially Q output of JK - FF  = 0 Now with the help of present state and next state table we can see what is happening in circuit.

  • Toggle: J = K = 1
  • Hold : J = K = 0

We see from table Q output of D-FF is going to next state input of JK-FF and the bits sequence produced is like 110110….. Including initial condition (0) we get output as 0110110110…. Hence answer is (A) part. Another Explanation: Here, it is given that JK flip flop will toggle when J = K = 1 and it will retain the output if J = K = 0. Also, the output of the D flip flop would remain the same as the input. So, we have Initial output : D = 1

JK = 0

After clock 1 : D = 0 (D gets 0 as input from initial output of JK, so output = 0)

JK = 1 (J = K = 1 from initial output of D, so output would be toggled from 0 to 1)

After clock 2 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as input from previous output of JK, so output = 1)

JK = 1 (J = K = 0 from previous output of D, so output would be retained to 1)

After clock 3 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 1)

JK = 0 (J = K = 1 from previous output of D, so output would be toggled from 1 to 0)

After clock 4 : D = 0 (D gets 0 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 0)

JK = 1 (J = K = 1 from previous output of D, so output would be toggled from 0 to 1)

After clock 5 : D = 1 (D gets 1 as inputfrom previous output of JK, so output = 1)

JK = 1 (J = K = 0 from previous output of D, so output would be retained to 1)

Thus, the bit sequence generated at the Q output of the JK flip flop will be 0110110...

questions.question: Let k = 2 ^n . A circuit is built by giving the output of an n-bit binary counter as input to an n-to-2 ^n bit decoder. This circuit is equivalent to a [GATE||2014||SET2||MCQ||1MARKS]
questions.question_html: Let k = 2n. A circuit is built by giving the output of an n-bit binary counter as input to an n-to-2n bit decoder. This circuit is equivalent to a [GATE||2014||SET2||MCQ||1MARKS]
questions.explanation:
A ring counter is a circular shift register in which only one flip-flop is set at a time and the rest is cleared.
A n x 2 ^n decoder is a combinational circuit with only one output line has one and all others (2 ^n -1) have zeros.
A n-bit binary Counter produces outputs from 0 to 2 ^n i.e 000...00 to 111...11 and repeats.
The n x 2 ^n Decoder gets the input (000..00 to 111...11 ) from the binary counter and only one output line has one and rest have zeros.
This circuit is equivalent to a 2 ^n - bit ring counter.
questions.explanation_html:
A ring counter is a circular shift register in which only one flip-flop is set at a time and the rest is cleared.

A n x 2n decoder is a combinational circuit with only one output line has one and all others (2n-1) have zeros.

A n-bit binary Counter produces outputs from 0 to 2n i.e 000...00 to 111...11 and repeats.

The n x 2n Decoder gets the input (000..00 to 111...11 ) from the binary counter and only one output line has one and rest have zeros.

This circuit is equivalent to a 2n - bit ring counter.

If there are m input lines and n output lines for a decoder that is used to uniquely address a byte addressable 1 KB RAM, then the minimum value of

m + n is ____.[GATE||2020 NAT || 2-mark ]
questions.explanation:
The size of the decoder required is 10x 2 ^10 i.e 10x 1024.
Each output line of the decoder is connected to one of the 1K(= 1024) rows of RAM.
Each row stores 1 Byte.

questions.explanation:
Why This Circuit is a D Latch: - The circuit uses a  multiplexer  and the selection signal S determines whether the output Q is updated (transparent mode, S=1) or retained (hold mode, S=0).
- There is  no clock signal  in this circuit. Without a clock, edge-triggering is not possible, so this circuit cannot function as a flip-flop.
- Instead, the circuit operates based on the level of S, which aligns with the functionality of a  D Latch .
questions.explanation_html:
Why This Circuit is a D Latch:

  • The circuit uses a multiplexer and the selection signal S determines whether the output Q is updated (transparent mode, S=1) or retained (hold mode, S=0).
  • There is no clock signal in this circuit. Without a clock, edge-triggering is not possible, so this circuit cannot function as a flip-flop.
  • Instead, the circuit operates based on the level of S, which aligns with the functionality of a D Latch.

questions.explanation:
Overflow indicates that the result was too large or too small to fit in the original data type.
Overflow flag indicates an overflow condition for a signed operation. Signed numbers are represented in two's complement representation.
The overflow occurs only when two positive number are added and the result is negative or two negative number are added and the result is positive. Otherwise, the sum has not overflowed.
Therefore, a XOR operation can quickly determine if an overflow condition exists. i.e.,
(A7 . B7 )⊕(S7) = A7 . B7 . S7‘ + A7‘ . B7‘ . S7 = 1

questions.explanation:
A = 01010 = 10
B= 11010 = -6
Option (b) = A - B
10 - (-6) = 10 + 6 = 16
The result 16 cannot be represented in 5 bits signed integers, which can range from -16 to 15:
5-bit binary for 16 would need more than 5 bits: 10000.
Hence, this operation results in an arithmetic overflow.

If both a and c are 0, then LHS = RHS.
If only one of a and c is 1, then it depends upon b. In this case also, LHS=RHS
Hence, option D is true .

questions.explanation:
Given expression is,
x _1  ⊕ x _2  ⊕ x _3  ⊕ x _4  = 0 -----(1)
A) x _1 x _2 x _3  x _4  = 0
Put x _1  = 1, x _2  = 1, x _3  = 1, x _4  = 1
The given equation will be zero, i.e.,
1 ⊕ 1 ⊕ 1 ⊕ 1 = 0
But,
x _1 x _2 x _3  x _4  ≠ 0
So, false.
B) x _1 x _3  + x _2  = 0
Put x _1  = 1, x _2  = 1, x _3  = 0 , x _4  = 0
The given equation will be zero, i.e.,
1 ⊕ 1 ⊕ 0 ⊕ 0 = 0
But,
x _1 x _3  + x _2  ≠ 0
So, false.
D) x _1  + x _2  + x _3  + x _4  = 0
Let x _1 =1, x _2 =1, x _3 =0, x _4 =0
The given equation will be zero, i.e.,
1 ⊕ 1 ⊕ 0 ⊕ 0 = 0
But,
x _1  + x _2  + x _3  + x _4  ≠ 0
So, false.

B) x1x3 + x2 = 0

D) x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 = 0

questions.explanation:
Number of possible minterms = 2 ^n .
Number of mutually exclusive pairs of minterms = 2 ^n-1 .
We have two possibilities for each pair of minterms: we can use one of the two minterms from each pair of minterms for the function, or we can use both.
Therefore number of functions = 2 x 2 x …. 2 ^n-1  times.
= 2 ^2^(n-1)

questions.question: A half adder is implemented with XOR and AND gates. A full adder is implemented with two half adders and one OR gate. The propagation delay of an XOR gate is twice that of an AND/OR gate. The propagation delay of an AND/OR gate is 1.2 microseconds. A 4-bit ripple-carry binary adder is implemented by using four full adders. The total propagation time of this 4-bit binary adder in microseconds is ____________. [GATE||2015||SET2||NAT||1MARKS]
questions.question_html: A half adder is implemented with XOR and AND gates. A full adder is implemented with two half adders and one OR gate. The propagation delay of an XOR gate is twice that of an AND/OR gate. The propagation delay of an AND/OR gate is 1.2 microseconds. A 4-bit ripple-carry binary adder is implemented by using four full adders. The total propagation time of this 4-bit binary adder in microseconds is ____________. [GATE||2015||SET2||NAT||1MARKS]
questions.explanation:
Ripple carry adder, If Delay of Logic GATEs are given then formula to calculate worst case delay is
T=(n-1)(T _AND + T _OR ) + Max ( T _Sum + T _Carry )
n= 4
T _and =T _or =1.2 Micro second
T _XOR= 2.4 Microsecond
Tsum= 2* T _Xor
T _carry = T _XOR + T _AND + T _OR
T _sum = 4.8 micro second
T _carry= 4.8 micro second
T= 3*2.4 micro second + 4.8 Microsecond
T=12 micro second
But the question says we use ripple-carry adder. So, each adder must wait for the full output from the previous adder. This would make the total delay= 4* 4.8=19.2 microsecond  and this is the key given by GATE, so obviously they meant this.

Ripple carry adder, If Delay of Logic GATEs are given then formula to calculate worst case delay is
T=(n-1)(TAND + TOR) + Max ( T Sum + T Carry)
n= 4
Tand=Tor=1.2 Micro second
TXOR=2.4 Microsecond
Tsum= 2* TXor
Tcarry = TXOR+ TAND+ TOR
Tsum= 4.8 micro second
Tcarry= 4.8 micro second
T= 3*2.4 micro second + 4.8 Microsecond
T=12 micro second

Let base of the number system is r.

(3r2 + r + 2) / 2r= (r+3+1/r)

(3r2 + r + 2) / 2r= (r2+3r+1) / r

(3r2 + r + 2) = (2r2+6r+2)

r2 -5r = 0

Therefore, r = 5

questions.title: PYQ-2014-1-Mark-Number-System
questions.question: Consider the equation (123) _5  = (x8) _y  with x and y as unknown. The number of possible solutions is __________. [GATE||2014||SET3||NAT||1MARKS]
questions.question_html: Consider the equation (123)5 = (x8)y with x and y as unknown. The number of possible solutions is __________. [GATE||2014||SET3||NAT||1MARKS]
questions.explanation:
First we have to fullfill all the conditios,
(123) _5  = (x8) _y
In R.H.S. since y is base so y should be greater than x and 8, i.e.,
y > x
y > 8
Now, to solve let's change all the above bases number into base 10 number,
5 ^2  × 1 +2 × 5 + 3 = y × x + 8
38 = xy + 8
xy = 30
⇒yx = 30
So the possible combinations are
(1,30), (2,15), (3,10), (5,6)
But we will reject (5,6) because it violates the condition (y > 8).
So, total solutions possible is 3.

F(P, Q, R, S) = ∑0, 2, 5, 7, 8, 10, 13, 15
The minterms 2, 7, 8 and 13 are ‘do not care’ terms. The minimal sum-of-products from for F is ? [GATE||2014||SET3||MCQ||1 MARKS]

questions.title: PYQ-2013-1-Mark-Number-System
questions.question: The smallest integer that can be represented by an 8-bit number in 2’s complement form is [GATE 2013 || MCQ || 1 MARKS ]
questions.question_html: The smallest integer that can be represented by an 8-bit number in 2’s complement form is [GATE 2013 || MCQ || 1 MARKS ]
questions.explanation:
The range of 8-bit signed numbers representable is – 2 ^n-1  to 2 ^n-1  -1.
The smallest 8-bit 2’s complement number is 1000 0000.
MSB is 1. So it is a negative number.
To know the magnitude again take 2’s complement of 1000 0000.
1000 0000
0111 1111 ← 1’s complement
1000 0000 ← 2’s complement (1’s complement +1)
= 128
–128 is 1000 0000 in 2’s complement representation.
